notes: The Unified Kill Chain (UKC) extends and modernizes the Lockheed Martin Cyber Kill Chain by incorporating MITRE ATT&CK tactics and addressing the original model's limitations. Developed by Paul Pols (2017, updated 2022). Bridges strategic and tactical views of adversary operations. Freely available at unifiedkillchain.com.

data.description: The UKC describes attack progression through three major phases (Initial Foothold, Network Propagation, Action on Objectives), each containing multiple tactics. Total 18 tactics across all phases.
data.notes: Key improvements over the original Kill Chain: cyclic model (tactics can repeat), includes insider threat paths, built on ATT&CK tactics, adds pivoting and social engineering. Version 2.2 is current.

data.note: No per-phase URL. UKC phases are documented on the main page and PDF.

examples.note: Phase 1: Getting into the network — recon through persistence (8 tactics)
Phase 2: Moving through the network — discovery through lateral movement (6 tactics)
Phase 3: Achieving goals — collection through impact (4 tactics)
